info.title: SARIF Multitool
info.description:
© Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.
absoluteuri                 Turn all relative Uris into absolute URIs (to be
used after rebaseUri is run)
apply-policy                Apply policies from SARIF log.
convert                     Convert a tool output log to SARIF format.
export-validation-config    Export validation rule options to an XML or JSON
file that can be edited and used to configure
subsequent analysis.
export-validation-rules     Export validation rules metadata to a SARIF or
SonarQube XML file.
file-work-items             Send SARIF results to a work item tracking system
such as GitHub or Azure DevOps
match-results-forward       Track results run over run by persisting IDs and
then matching them forward
merge                       Merge multiple SARIF files into one.
page                        Extract a subset of results from a source SARIF
file.
query                       Find the matching subset of a SARIF file and
output it or log it.
rebaseuri                   Rebase the URIs in one or more sarif files.
rewrite                     Enrich a SARIF file with additional data.
suppress                    Enrich a SARIF file with additional data.
validate                    Validate a SARIF file against the schema and
against additional correctness rules.
help                        Display more information on a specific command.
version                     Display version information.

commands.name: absoluteuri
commands.description:
Turn all relative Uris into absolute URIs (to be
used after rebaseUri is run)

options.name: --recurse
options.description:
(Default: false) Recursively select
subdirectories in paths.

options.name: --output-directory
options.description:
A directory to output the transformed files to.
If absent, each transformed file is written to
the same directory as the corresponding input
file.

options.name: --log
options.description:
Optionally present data, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in double
quotes, for governing output files. Valid values
include ForceOverwrite, Inline, PrettyPrint,
Minify or Optimize.

options.name: --insert
options.description:
Optionally present data, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in double
quotes, that should be inserted into the log
file. Valid values include Hashes, TextFiles,
BinaryFiles, EnvironmentVariables,
RegionSnippets, ContextRegionSnippets,
ContextRegionSnippetPartialFingerprints, Guids,
VersionControlDetails, and
NondeterministicProperties.

options.name: --remove
options.description:
Optionally present data, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in double
quotes, that should be not be persisted to or
which should be removed from the log file. Valid
values include Hashes, TextFiles, BinaryFiles,
EnvironmentVariables, RegionSnippets,
ContextRegionSnippets, Guids,
VersionControlDetails, and
NondeterministicProperties.

options.name: --uriBaseIds
options.description:
Key + value pairs, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in double
quotes, that defines a uriBaseId and its
corresponding local file path. E.g.,
SRC=c:\src;TEST=c:\test

options.name: --sarif-output-version
options.description:
(Default: Current) The SARIF version of the
output log file. Valid values are OneZeroZero
and Current

options.name: --threads
options.description:
A count of threads that should be used for
multithreaded operations.

options.name: --insert-property
options.description:
JSON path + property values, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in double
quotes, that should be inserted into the output
log. Currently, only paths that point to a
version control provenance property bag is
supported, e.g.,
'runs[0].invocations[1].versionControlProvenance
.properties.myProperty=myValue'.

options.name: --automation-id
options.description:
An id that will be persisted to the
'Run.AutomationDetails.Id' property. See section
'3.17.3' of the SARIF specification for more
information.

options.name: --automation-guid
options.description:
A guid that will be persisted to the
'Run.AutomationDetails.Guid' property. See
section '3.17.4' of the SARIF specification for
more information.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description:
Display version information.
<files> (pos. 0)              Required. Files to process (wildcards ? and *
allowed).

options.name: --output
options.description: A file path to the generated SARIF log.

commands.name: convert
commands.description: Convert a tool output log to SARIF format.

options.name: --tool
options.description:
Required. The tool format of the input file.
Must be one of: AndroidStudio, ClangAnalyzer,
ClangTidy, CppCheck, ContrastSecurity,
FlawFinder, Fortify, FortifyFpr, FxCop, Hdf,
PREfast, Pylint, SemmleQL, StaticDriverVerifier,
TSLint, or a tool format for which a plugin
assembly provides the converter.

options.name: --plugin-assembly-path
options.description:
Path to plugin assembly containing converter
types.

options.name: --normalize-for-github
options.description:
(Deprecated. Use --normalize-for-ghas instead.)
Normalize converted output to conform to GitHub
Advanced Security (GHAS) code scanning ingestion
requirements.

options.name: --normalize-for-ghas
options.description:
Normalize converted output to conform to GitHub
Advanced Security (GHAS) code scanning ingestion
requirements.

commands.name: export-validation-config
commands.description:
Export validation rule options to an XML or JSON
file that can be edited and used to configure
subsequent analysis.

options.name: --format
options.description:
(Default: Json) The file format to persist settings to (if the
format cannot be inferred from the file name extension).

options.name: --plugin
options.description:
Plugin paths, expressed as a semicolon-delimited list enclosed
in double quotes, that will be invoked to retrieve rule
options.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description:
Display version information.
value pos. 0    Required. Output path for exported analysis options

commands.name: export-validation-rules
commands.description:
Export validation rules metadata to a SARIF or
SonarQube XML file.

options.name: --plugin
options.description:
Plugin paths, expressed as a semicolon-delimited list enclosed
in double quotes, that will be invoked to retrieve rule
metadata.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description:
Display version information.
value pos. 0    Required. Output path for exported analysis options.
Use a .json or .sarif extension to produce SARIF.
Use .xml to produce a SonarQube rule descriptor file.
Use .md to produce a markdow rule descriptor file.

commands.name: file-work-items
commands.description:
Send SARIF results to a work item tracking system
such as GitHub or Azure DevOps

options.name: --host-uri
options.description:
The absolute URI of the project in which the
work items are to be filed, for example
https://dev.azure.com/{org}/{project} or
https://github.com/{org}/{repo}.

options.name: --split
options.description:
(Default: None) Apply a splitting strategy to
each input log file in order to break the file
into multiple bugs. Must be one of None, PerRun,
PerResult, PerRunPerRule, PerRunPerTargetPerRule
or PerRunPerTarget. By default ('None'), no
splitting strategy is applied (i.e. each input
file will be filed as a single bug).

options.name: --sync-workitem-metadata
options.description:
Enrich work item to be created with existing
work item metadata for any result workItemUri.

options.name: --file-unchanged
options.description:
File work items for both new and unchanged
baseline state results. If an unchanged result
already has an associated work item, a new work
item will not be created.

options.name: --configuration
options.description:
A path to an XML configuration file that will be
used to drive work item creation.

options.name: --no-validate
options.description:
Do not validate the SARIF log file before
filing.

options.name: --property-name
options.description:
The property name that will be used to split per
Fingerprint or per PropertyBag

options.name: --output
options.description: A file path to the generated SARIF log.

options.name: --log
options.description:

commands.name: match-results-forward
commands.description:
Track results run over run by persisting IDs and
then matching them forward

options.name: --previous
options.description:
Path to a SARIF log containing the previous set
of results with result matching annotations on a
target (or empty if no previous log exists)

options.name: --output-file-path
options.description:
File path to output the annotated SARIF log with
result matching information.  Defaults to
<currentFile>-annotated.sarif

options.description:

commands.name: merge
commands.description: Merge multiple SARIF files into one.

options.name: --output-file
options.description:
(Default: merged.sarif) File name to write
merged content to.

options.name: --merge-empty-logs
options.description:
Merge log files with no results into the final
log.

options.name: --split
options.description:
(Default: None) Apply a splitting strategy to
the merged file. Must be one of None or PerRule.
By default ('None'), no splitting strategy is
applied (i.e. all input files will be merged
into a single log).

options.name: --merge-runs
options.description:
Merge runs of the same tool + version
combination (requires eliding run-specific
details such as invocations data.

options.name: --recurse
options.description:
(Default: false) Recursively select
subdirectories in paths.

options.name: --output-directory
options.description:
A directory to output the transformed files to.
If absent, each transformed file is written to
the same directory as the corresponding input
file.

commands.name: page
commands.description: Extract a subset of results from a source SARIF

options.name: --run-index
options.description: (Default: 0) 0-based index of the run to copy.

options.name: --index
options.description: Required. 0-based index of first result to include.

options.name: --count
options.description: Required. Number of results from index to include.

options.name: --force
options.description:
(Default: false) Force overwrite of output file if it
exists.

options.name: --output
options.description: Required. A file path of the SARIF subset output file.

options.name: --map-ratio
options.description:
(Default: 0.01) Target map size relative to file size
(0.01 is 1%)

options.name: --minify
options.description:
(Default: false) Produce compact JSON output rather
than more readable, expanded form.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description:
Display version information.
<inputFile> (pos. 0)    Required. A path to a SARIF file to process.

commands.name: query
commands.description:
Find the matching subset of a SARIF file and
output it or log it.

options.name: --expression
options.description:
Required. Result Expression to Evaluate
(ex: (BaselineState != 'Unchanged'))

options.name: --write-to-console
options.description:
(Default: false) Whether to write
matching results to the console

options.name: --non-zero-exit-code-if-count-over
options.description:
(Default: -1) Whether to return a
non-zero exit code if the count exceeds
a threshold

options.name: --return-count
options.description:
(Default: false) Exit Code is the count
of matching results

options.name: --force
options.description:
(Default: false) Force overwrite of
output file if it exists.

options.name: --minify
options.description:
(Default: false) Produce compact JSON
output rather than more readable,
expanded form.

options.name: --output
options.description:
A file path of the SARIF subset output
file, if desired.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description:
Display version information.
<inputFile> (pos. 0)                  Required. A path to a SARIF file to
process.

commands.name: rebaseuri
commands.description: Rebase the URIs in one or more sarif files.

options.name: --base-path-value
options.description:
Required. Base path value to use while rebasing
all paths.  E.x. 'C:\bld\1234\bin\'

options.name: --base-path-token
options.description:
Required. Variable to use for the base path
token (e.x. 'SRCROOT')

options.name: --rebase-relative-uris
options.description:
(Default: false) All relative uris will be
rebased to the base-path-value if true. Default
is false.

options.name: --recurse
options.description:
(Default: false) Recursively select
subdirectories in paths.

options.name: --output-directory
options.description:
A directory to output the transformed files to.
If absent, each transformed file is written to
the same directory as the corresponding input
file.

commands.name: rewrite
commands.description: Enrich a SARIF file with additional data.

options.name: --sort-results
options.description:
(Default: false) Sort results in the final
output file.

options.name: --normalize-for-ghas
options.description:
Normalize SARIF to conform to GitHub Advanced
Security (GHAS) code scanning ingestion
requirements.

options.name: --base-path-value
options.description:
Base path value to use while rebasing all paths.
E.x. 'C:\bld\1234\bin\'

options.name: --base-path-token
options.description:
Variable to use for the base path token (e.x.
'SRCROOT')

options.name: --rebase-relative-uris
options.description:
(Default: false) All relative uris will be
rebased to the base-path-value if true. Default
is false.

options.name: --output
options.description: A file path to the generated SARIF log.

options.name: --log
options.description:
Optionally present data, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in double
quotes, for governing output files. Valid values
include ForceOverwrite, Inline, PrettyPrint,
Minify or Optimize.

options.name: --insert
options.description:
Optionally present data, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in double
quotes, that should be inserted into the log
file. Valid values include Hashes, TextFiles,
BinaryFiles, EnvironmentVariables,
RegionSnippets, ContextRegionSnippets,
ContextRegionSnippetPartialFingerprints, Guids,
VersionControlDetails, and
NondeterministicProperties.

options.name: --remove
options.description:
Optionally present data, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in double
quotes, that should be not be persisted to or
which should be removed from the log file. Valid
values include Hashes, TextFiles, BinaryFiles,
EnvironmentVariables, RegionSnippets,
ContextRegionSnippets, Guids,
VersionControlDetails, and
NondeterministicProperties.

options.name: --uriBaseIds
options.description:
Key + value pairs, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in double
quotes, that defines a uriBaseId and its
corresponding local file path. E.g.,
SRC=c:\src;TEST=c:\test

options.name: --sarif-output-version
options.description:
(Default: Current) The SARIF version of the
output log file. Valid values are OneZeroZero
and Current

options.name: --threads
options.description:
A count of threads that should be used for
multithreaded operations.

options.name: --insert-property
options.description:
JSON path + property values, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in double
quotes, that should be inserted into the output
log. Currently, only paths that point to a
version control provenance property bag is
supported, e.g.,
'runs[0].invocations[1].versionControlProvenance
.properties.myProperty=myValue'.

options.name: --automation-id
options.description:
An id that will be persisted to the
'Run.AutomationDetails.Id' property. See section
'3.17.3' of the SARIF specification for more
information.

options.name: --automation-guid
options.description:
A guid that will be persisted to the
'Run.AutomationDetails.Guid' property. See
section '3.17.4' of the SARIF specification for
more information.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description:
Display version information.
<inputFile> (pos. 0)          Required. A path to a file to process.

commands.name: suppress
commands.description: Enrich a SARIF file with additional data.

options.name: --justification
options.description:
Required. A string that provides the rationale
for the suppressions

options.name: --alias
options.description:
The account name associated with the
suppression.

options.name: --guids
options.description:
A UUID that will be associated with a
suppression.

options.name: --timestamps
options.description:
The property 'timeUtc' that will be associated
with a suppression.

options.name: --expiryInDays
options.description:
The property 'expiryUtc' that will be associated
with a suppression from the 'timeUtc'.

options.name: --status
options.description:
The status that will be used in the suppression.
Valid values include Accepted and UnderReview.

options.name: --output
options.description: A file path to the generated SARIF log.

options.name: --log

semicolon-delimited list enclosed in double

multithreaded operations.

JSON path + property values, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in double
quotes, that should be inserted into the output
log. Currently, only paths that point to a

.properties.myProperty=myValue'.

options.name: --automation-id
options.description:
An id that will be persisted to the
'Run.AutomationDetails.Id' property. See section

information.

options.name: --automation-guid
options.description:
A guid that will be persisted to the
'Run.AutomationDetails.Guid' property. See

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description:
Display version information.
<inputFile> (pos. 0)          Required. A path to a file to process.

commands.name: validate
commands.description:
Validate a SARIF file against the schema and
against additional correctness rules.

options.name: --json-schema
options.description: Path to the SARIF JSON schema.

options.name: --update-inputs-to-current-sarif
options.description:
Update any SARIF v1 or prerelease v2 files
to the current SARIF v2 format.

options.name: --output
options.description:
File path to which analysis output will be
written.

options.name: --recurse
options.description:
Recurse into subdirectories when
evaluating file specifier arguments.

options.name: --config
options.description:
Path to policy file that will be used to
configure analysis. This defaults to
'default.configuration.xml' beside the
main tool; passing value of 'default' or
removing that file will configure the tool
to use its built-in settings.

options.name: --output-config
options.description:
Path to a policy file to which all
analysis settings from the current run
will be saved.

options.name: --quiet
options.description:
Suppress all console output (except for
catastrophic tool runtime or configuration
errors).

options.name: --environment
options.description:
Log machine environment details of run to
output file. WARNING: This option records
potentially sensitive information (such as
all environment variable values) to any
emitted log.

options.name: --plugin
options.description:
Plugin paths, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in
double quotes, that points to plugin(s)
that should drive analysis for all
configured scan targets.

options.name: --invocation-properties
options.description:
Properties of the Invocation object to
log, expressed as a semicolon-delimited
list enclosed in double quotes. NOTE:
StartTime and EndTime are always logged.

options.name: --rich-return-code
options.description:
Emit a 'rich' return code consisting of a
bitfield of conditions (as opposed to 0 or
1 indicating success or failure.

options.name: --trace
options.description:
Execution traces, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in
double quotes, that should be emitted to
the console and log file (if appropriate).
Valid values: ScanTime.

options.name: --level
options.description:
Failure levels, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in
double quotes, that is used to filter the
scan results. Valid values: Error, Warning
and Note.

options.name: --kind
options.description:
Result kinds, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in
double quotes, that is used to filter the
scan results. Valid values: Fail (for
literal scan results), Pass, Review, Open,
NotApplicable and Informational.

options.name: --baseline
options.description: A SARIF file to be used as baseline.

options.name: --etw
options.description:
A file path to which all ETW events for
the session will be saved.

options.name: --post-uri
options.description:
A URI to which the SARIF log file will be
posted.

options.name: --max-file-size-in-kb
options.description:
The maximum file size (in kilobytes) that
will be analyzed.

options.name: --timeout-in-seconds
options.description: A timeout value expressed in seconds.

options.name: --deny-regex
options.description:
A regular expression used to suppress
scanning for any file or directory path
that matches the regex.

options.name: --rule-kind
options.description:
Specify the kind(s) of rules that should
be run.

options.name: --log
options.description:
Optionally present data, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in
double quotes, for governing output files.
Valid values include ForceOverwrite,
Inline, PrettyPrint, Minify or Optimize.

options.name: --insert
options.description:
Optionally present data, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in
double quotes, that should be inserted
into the log file. Valid values include
Hashes, TextFiles, BinaryFiles,
EnvironmentVariables, RegionSnippets,
ContextRegionSnippets,
ContextRegionSnippetPartialFingerprints,
Guids, VersionControlDetails, and
NondeterministicProperties.

options.name: --remove
options.description:

semicolon-delimited list enclosed in
double quotes, that should be not be
persisted to or which should be removed
from the log file. Valid values include
Hashes, TextFiles, BinaryFiles,
EnvironmentVariables, RegionSnippets,
ContextRegionSnippets, Guids,
VersionControlDetails, and
NondeterministicProperties.

options.name: --uriBaseIds

semicolon-delimited list enclosed in
double quotes, that defines a uriBaseId
and its corresponding local file path.
E.g., SRC=c:\src;TEST=c:\test

the output log file. Valid values are
OneZeroZero and Current

multithreaded operations.

enance.properties.myProperty=myValue'.

options.name: --automation-id

An id that will be persisted to the
'Run.AutomationDetails.Id' property. See

specification for more information.

options.name: --automation-guid
options.description:

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description:
Display version information.
value pos. 0                        One or more specifiers to a file,
directory, or filter pattern that resolves
to one or more binaries to analyze.

commands.name: help
commands.description: Display more information on a specific command.
